project.name: crabcc
project.tagline: Symbol index for AI coding agents — 47–4400× faster than `grep -rn`, 5–100× faster than ripgrep, 85% fewer bytes

session-origin.first-user-prompt: make it more performant and more token savings :D, add example usage markdown both cli+mcp, also we should bench the 'tool', the cli vs grep and find+ls+grep commands no? first layer bench, then I'll do the 'real', claude session test manually later
session-origin.session-arc:
Starting state: v0.1 with sym/refs/callers/outline/files (TODO grep), basic MCP, basic skill. 60 tests. Single grep-based bench harness (Claude session, counted output tokens only — gave misleading "crabcc costs more" result because it ignored cache costs).
Through-arc: token-shaping flags + early-stop, snippet trim, files command, first-layer (CLI-vs-CLI) bench harness producing PNG charts, README pimp, logo (4 variants), MCP+skill+command global install, optimised release profile, dev-fast profile, expanded test profile, devcontainer, .dockerignore, Taskfile, manpage, GitHub Actions CI with cargo-nextest + JUnit XML artifacts, GitHub Actions release with multi-arch UPX-compressed builds, ARCHITECTURE.md with mermaid diagrams, watch sidecar (notify-debouncer-mini + worker thread + feedback-loop guard), graph sidecar (BFS over caller/ callee adjacency + JSON cache), thread-safe Store (WAL+Send+Mutex pattern), SQLite tuning (5 hot-path indexes + 5 PRAGMAs), worktree isolation tested, coverage tests (60 → 102), clippy strict + dead-code removal, MemPalace research (1027-line port plan + sqlite-vec recommendation), FSST research (272-line v2.0 candidate report), GitHub issue + comment attached, v2.0 milestone with 6 epics. Currently: v1.0.0 staged, ready to tag.

highlight-feature.name: Token-shaping flags
highlight-feature.details: refs/callers gain --limit, --files-only, --count modes with early-stop on the per-file walk. --count collapses a 16k-token result set to ~3 tokens.

highlight-feature.name: crabcc files
highlight-feature.details: SQLite-backed file listing with --under PFX, --lang LANG, --ext EXT, --limit N. Replaces ls -R / find -name. ~600× faster than find on a Rails monorepo.

highlight-feature.name: Watch sidecar
highlight-feature.details: notify-debouncer-mini-based FS watchdog. Worker thread (named "crabcc-watch"); debounced events (default 500ms) trigger incremental refresh; feedback-loop guard skips events under .crabcc/. spawn() returns a join-able WatchHandle with stop() + block_until_done().

highlight-feature.name: Graph sidecar
highlight-feature.details: Call-graph BFS persisted to .crabcc/graph.json. crabcc graph-build builds; crabcc graph NAME --dir callers|callees --depth N queries. MCP tool "graph" mirrors the CLI.

highlight-feature.name: Tantivy fuzzy + prefix sidecar
highlight-feature.details: FuzzyTermQuery (Levenshtein dist 2) + RegexQuery (case-insensitive prefix) over symbol names. .crabcc/tantivy/ — rebuilt on crabcc index, manually via crabcc fts-rebuild.

highlight-feature.name: Token-savings tracker
highlight-feature.details: Append-only JSONL log at ~/.crabcc/usage.log. crabcc track shows session/24h/all-time saved tokens by op. Heuristic estimates conservative.

crate.name: crabcc-core
crate.module: ignore-aware FS walk; 4 tests covering basic/.ignore/dotfile/empty cases
tree-sitter symbol extraction per language; 9 tests
ast-grep callers (bare + method-receiver); 11 tests including snippet trim
rusqlite Connection + WAL/foreign_keys/mmap_size=30GB/cache_size=16MB/temp_store=MEMORY/busy_timeout=2s pragmas; compile-time Send assertion; analyze() method; 10 tests
full + incremental indexing logic; 7 tests including git_worktree_isolation (#[ignore]'d due to tempdir mtime granularity)
sym/refs/callers with Mode { Hits/FilesOnly/Count } and early-stop; 13 tests
call-graph sidecar (BFS, JSON cache, cycle-safe); 5 tests
FS-watch worker thread with feedback-loop guard; 4 unit tests + 1 ignored e2e
token-savings telemetry with HOME_LOCK Mutex for parallel test isolation; 7 tests

crate.name: crabcc-cli
crate.details: clap CLI with 13 subcommands: index/refresh/sym/refs/callers/outline/files/grep/fuzzy/prefix/fts-rebuild/track/watch/graph-build/graph. SIGPIPE-safe. tracing-subscriber EnvFilter defaults to "warn".

crate.name: crabcc-mcp
crate.details: JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio server. Implements initialize/tools/list/tools/call. 9 tools: sym/refs/callers/outline/files/index/refresh/fuzzy/prefix/graph. Tests cover parse_mode (5), tools/list shape (2), each tool's happy-path + error-path (10).

infra-shipped.ci: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets, cargo nextest run --profile ci (with JUnit XML upload as 30-day-retention artifact per matrix entry), cargo build --release, smoke test (sym/callers/files/outline/watch/prefix on a tiny TS fixture). Matrix: ubuntu-latest + macos-latest. CURRENTLY GREEN.

Triggers on v*.*.* tag. Multi-arch matrix: linux x86_64/aarch64 (cross), macOS x86_64/aarch64. UPX --best --lzma compression (skips macOS aarch64 — UPX limitation). sha256 sums. softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with generate_release_notes. PRE-RELEASE if tag contains '-'.
infra-shipped.devcontainer: Rust 1-bookworm base + ripgrep + fd-find + jq + matplotlib. postCreate: build crabcc, install to /usr/local/bin. VS Code extensions: rust-analyzer, even-better-toml, lldb, crates.
infra-shipped.nextest-config: default + ci profiles. ci has 2-retry backoff, 60s slow-timeout, JUnit reporter.
infra-shipped.install-script: curl|bash. Detects host OS+arch. Downloads release tarball, verifies sha256 if available, installs to ~/.local/bin or /usr/local/bin (sudo fallback).
infra-shipped.homebrew-formula: Tap formula skeleton. Multi-arch URLs. brew test does index + sym round-trip. Version + sha256 placeholders for release.yml to fill in.

docs-shipped.doc: Centered logo + 8 GitHub badges; quick demo block; collapsible TOC; install/usage/token-shaping/bench-with-charts/architecture/when-not-to-use/status sections; roadmap table linking v2.0 milestone issues #1–6.

Engineer-facing deep dive with mermaid diagrams: crate dependency graph, full data-flow per command, threading sequence diagram for watch sidecar, SQLite ER diagram, MCP wire-protocol sequence diagram, runbooks for adding subcommands/languages/sidecars, build-profile rationale, performance shape, gantt roadmap, troubleshooting table.

Full Rust-port plan for MemPalace as crabcc memory v2.0 subcommand. 8 parts + 6 appendices. Vector-store comparison (sqlite-vec, arroy, lancedb, hnsw_rs, usearch, qdrant, instant-distance, milvus-lite-rs) with decision matrix. sqlite-vec implementation walkthrough with full Rust code. 12 fine-tuning levers. Performance/scale table 1k → 5M drawers. 11-step implementation checklist.
FSST string-compression integration plan for v2.0. Per-column gain estimates (pessimistic/optimistic). Schema migration strategy (additive, backward-compatible). 6-phase rollout. Risks register.
Index for 11 per-topic example files plus 2 cheatsheets (CLI.md, MCP.md).
indexing, sym, refs, callers, outline, files, fuzzy-prefix, jq-pipelines, track, mcp-setup, wire-protocol — each with bench numbers + when-not-to-use.
Hand-written troff manpage covering NAME/SYNOPSIS/DESCRIPTION/COMMANDS/OPTIONS/FILES/ENVIRONMENT/EXIT STATUS/EXAMPLES/SEE ALSO/AUTHOR/BUGS.
9-task CLI-vs-CLI A/B comparing crabcc against grep/find/cat AND ripgrep. Headline: 85% fewer bytes, 182× faster aggregate wall-time.
Log-scale grouped bar chart of output-bytes per task, 3 columns (crabcc, ripgrep, grep).
Log-scale bar chart of speedup factor, color-coded wins/losses with per-bar speedup labels.

visual-shipped.logo: Full geometry: body + 6 legs + 2 claws (with `<` / `>` bracket notches) + 2 eyes shaped as `c` letterforms (the cc of crabcc) + smile + shell dimples. Orange gradient.
Favicon-style: body + claws + eyes only. Reads at 16×16.
Single-color via currentColor. Recolours from surrounding text. Eyes simplified to dots.
Horizontal with wordmark + tagline. Subtle gradient background. Suitable for OpenGraph / social cards.

bench-numbers.aggregate: Total bytes: crabcc 257.5k, rg 248.0k, grep 1.9M. Saved bytes vs grep: 85% — about 411k input tokens per equivalent batch. Total wall-time: crabcc 1.21s, rg 51.3s, grep 220.5s. Speedup vs grep: 182×. Speedup vs ripgrep: ~42× weighted by task.

integration-points.task-items: Mirror folder with README, CHANGELOG, SESSION-HANDOFF.xml, .tasks (4-dev × 2-week sprint plan), docs/, bench/, examples/, assets/, man/.

issue.title: Add FSST string compression for symbol bodies / drawer text (v2.0)

issues-open.issue: Body has high-level summary + acceptance criteria + risks. First comment: 6-phase recommended path + pessimistic gain estimate. Linked to docs/RESEARCH-fsst.md.

issue.title: Epic: crabcc memory — local-first AI memory layer (MemPalace port)

issues-open.issue: New crates/crabcc-memory crate. Reuses 70% of plumbing. Release gate: ≥96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval.

issue.title: Epic: edges-at-extract — drop graph build from O(n²) to O(n)

issues-open.issue: Populate edges table during extraction. Pure-SQL caller queries. Bench gate: callers latency <50ms.

issue.title: Epic: language coverage — add Go, Python, Rust extractors
issue.labels: epic, feature, lang:go, lang:python, lang:rust, v2.0

issues-open.issue: ~3 days each end-to-end. Cross-lang test fixtures. examples/languages.md.

issue.title: Epic: distribution + DX — install script, brew tap, mdBook docs site, demos

issues-open.issue: Install script DONE. Homebrew tap, mdBook site, asciinema demos remaining.

issue.title: CI optimizations — caching, sccache, reduced job count, faster matrix

issues-open.issue: Levers: sccache, smarter cache key, cancel-in-progress concurrency, single-OS fmt/clippy. Acceptance: CI <2 min on no-op PR.

commits-this-session.commit: add token-shaping flags + crabcc files; optimize release profile
add tests for new modes, files cmd, MCP parse_mode, snippet trim (+18 tests)
add release infra: README, CHANGELOG, CI, devcontainer, manpage, bench, examples
add .dockerignore; point repository URLs at peterlodri-sec
add watch + graph sidecars; tune SQLite; clippy clean; +14 coverage tests
add SVG logo + MemPalace research report
ci: add cargo-nextest + JUnit XML test report as build artifact
docs: add FSST integration research (v2.0 candidate)
ci: build release binary before smoke test; broaden smoke coverage
docs: link ARCHITECTURE.md from README; refresh CHANGELOG
distribution: add install.sh + Homebrew formula skeleton
docs: pimp README + add 3 logo variants (small/mono/banner)
docs: embed bench PNG charts in README; link full report

run.reason: smoke step missing release build (fixed in 679979c)

next-steps.step: Tag v1.0.0 on the bc24ff2 commit. `git tag v1.0.0 -m "crabcc 1.0.0" && git push origin v1.0.0`. release.yml then fires multi-arch UPX-compressed build, sha256 sums, GitHub Release with auto-generated notes.
Verify release-yml run completes green; download an artifact and smoke-test it on a clean machine (or in the devcontainer).
Create the peterlodri-sec/homebrew-tap repo (issue #5). Move contrib/homebrew/crabcc.rb to it. Add release.yml step that updates version+sha256 in the tap formula on each tag push.
Set up the docs site via mdBook (issue #5). Pulls README + ARCHITECTURE + RESEARCH-* + CHANGELOG + examples/. Auto-deploy from main to gh-pages.
Apply the 6 CI-optimization levers from issue #6 (sccache, cancel-in-progress, single-OS fmt/clippy, smarter cache key). Target: <2 min CI wall-time on no-op PRs.
Begin v2.0 sprint per task-items/crabcc/.tasks. 4 senior devs, 4 parallel tracks (memory, edges-at-extract, languages, polish/release).
asciinema recording → GIF → embed in README. Marketing assets (tweet, LinkedIn) via the gh-beast workflow.
cargo publish (crabcc-core → crabcc-mcp → crabcc-cli) once we're confident the public API is stable. crates.io badge would replace the github-release badge.
Convert the 1 ignored e2e watch test + 1 ignored worktree test to use the filetime crate for nanosecond mtime, re-enable.
grep subcommand (crabcc-cli main.rs has a TODO stub) — implement via the grep crate, annotate hits with enclosing symbol.

conventions-for-future-agents.commit-style: Lowercase, present-tense imperative, single concept per commit, optional colon-subtitle. Examples: "add token-shaping flags + crabcc files; optimize release profile", "ci: build release binary before smoke test; broaden smoke coverage", "docs: add ARCHITECTURE.md (engineer-facing, mermaid diagrams)". Use HEREDOC for multi-line commit-message bodies.
conventions-for-future-agents.emojis: User asked for emojis only in ARCHITECTURE.md. No emojis in code, tests, configs, or other docs unless explicitly requested.
conventions-for-future-agents.test-isolation: track.rs tests use HOME_LOCK Mutex to serialise $HOME mutation across parallel cargo tests. Apply the same pattern when adding tests that mutate process-wide state.
conventions-for-future-agents.store-thread-safety: Store: Send (compile-time asserted). NOT Sync. Wrap in Arc<Mutex<Store>> to share across threads. WAL mode covers concurrent reads through separate connections.
conventions-for-future-agents.gitignore: .crabcc/, target/, Cargo.lock, bench/results/ (with !REPORT.md, !*.png allowlist for the visual artifacts).
conventions-for-future-agents.skill-and-command: Symlinks at ~/.claude/skills/crabcc/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/commands/crabcc-init.md point at the repo. Repo is the source of truth.
conventions-for-future-agents.ci-quality-gate: cargo fmt --check + clippy --all-targets -D warnings + nextest + smoke. Don't merge red.

test.reason: passes manually via /tmp/test_worktree.sh, races with tempdir mtime granularity in cargo's parallel runner. Fix: switch to filetime crate for nanosecond precision.
FS events are coarse-grained and racy across CI runners; deliberately #[ignore]'d. The deterministic should_trigger_* and handle_stop_signals_worker tests cover the logic that matters.

crabcc-summary.how-to-resume: A fresh agent picking up the polish loop should: 1. Read README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, CHANGELOG.md, this file. 2. Skim docs/RESEARCH-mempalace.md and docs/RESEARCH-fsst.md. 3. Run `gh issue list --milestone v2.0` to see current state. 4. Pick the next priority from the <next-steps> section above. 5. Local: `task ci` to verify clean before any new work. 6. Each commit: `task fmt-check && task lint && task test`.
